channel.title: High-Profile Athlete Injury Report
channel.description: Daily-updated feed of high-profile athlete injuries across NFL, NBA, WNBA, MLB, NHL, top soccer leagues, tennis, golf, F1, boxing/UFC, cricket, Olympic sports, and NCAA D1.

item.title: Kierstan Bell (Basketball) — Right leg injury (specific diagnosis not disclosed in available reporting)
item.description: Kierstan Bell (Las Vegas Aces (WNBA)) — Right leg injury (specific diagnosis not disclosed in available reporting). Expected duration: Ruled out for the Aces' July 13 game vs. the Indiana Fever; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Aces without a rotation forward who had recently moved into the starting lineup, adding to the team's list of unavailable players.

item.title: Satou Sabally (Basketball) — Concussion protocol after being elbowed in the face by Aces forward NaLyssa Smith while contesting a rebound; symptoms had delayed onset
item.description: Satou Sabally (New York Liberty (WNBA)) — Concussion protocol after being elbowed in the face by Aces forward NaLyssa Smith while contesting a rebound; symptoms had delayed onset. Expected duration: Downgraded from questionable to out; remains in concussion protocol with no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Liberty without their All-Star forward for multiple games as she works through the NBA/WNBA-mandated concussion protocol; notable given she also missed time with a concussion in the 2025 WNBA Finals.

item.title: Angel Reese (Basketball) — Lower leg/ankle injury sustained falling under the basket late in a win over the Seattle Storm
item.description: Angel Reese (Atlanta Dream (WNBA)) — Lower leg/ankle injury sustained falling under the basket late in a win over the Seattle Storm. Expected duration: Listed questionable for the Dream's game vs. the Portland Fire; no firm return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Puts a third-straight All-Star selection and Dream's first year with Reese (acquired in an offseason trade from Chicago) at risk of interruption during a stretch where the team is trying to build momentum after a five-game losing streak.

item.title: Brionna Jones (Basketball) — Meniscus tear in the right knee suffered in late January while playing overseas for USK Prague; underwent knee surgery at Emory Hospital
item.description: Brionna Jones (Atlanta Dream (WNBA)) — Meniscus tear in the right knee suffered in late January while playing overseas for USK Prague; underwent knee surgery at Emory Hospital. Expected duration: Ruled out to begin the 2026 season and remains sidelined into mid-July; not yet cleared for 5-on-5 drills and has no firm return timetable, though the team says she is expected to make a full recovery. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Four-time All-Star center has yet to play a game for the Dream this season, a significant frontcourt loss to start the year.

item.title: Leonie Fiebich (Basketball) — Left foot soreness
item.description: Leonie Fiebich (New York Liberty (WNBA)) — Left foot soreness. Expected duration: Ruled out for a second straight game, having missed her first game since May 25; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Liberty forward sidelined for consecutive games amid a compressed schedule that includes a quick turnaround for a game in Toronto.

item.title: Johan Manzambi (Soccer) — Left-knee injury suffered in training on the eve of the Round of 16 match vs. Colombia
item.description: Johan Manzambi (Switzerland National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Left-knee injury suffered in training on the eve of the Round of 16 match vs. Colombia. Expected duration: Missed the Round of 16 win over Colombia and ruled out of the quarterfinal vs. Argentina; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Major blow to Switzerland's attack: with Manzambi on the pitch during the group stage the team averaged 3.6 goals per 90 minutes (3 goals, 2 assists for him in 200 minutes); without him, Switzerland have scored just once and averaged 1.4 xG per 90 over 280 minutes.

item.title: Marc Guehi (Soccer) — Hamstring strain picked up in England's Round of 16 win over Mexico
item.description: Marc Guehi (England National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Hamstring strain picked up in England's Round of 16 win over Mexico. Expected duration: Missed a training session in Kansas City but returned to training Friday and was confirmed available for the quarterfinal vs. Norway. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Created a late fitness scare for England's back line ahead of the quarterfinal, with Newcastle's Dan Burn on standby to deputize had Guehi been unavailable.

item.title: Nick Kurtz (Baseball) — Right thumb capsule sprain
item.description: Nick Kurtz (Athletics (MLB)) — Right thumb capsule sprain. Expected duration: Placed on the 10-day injured list, retroactive to July 10. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Keeps the 2025 AL Rookie of the Year and scheduled AL All-Star starting first baseman (.266, 20 HR, 66 RBI, MLB-leading 76 walks) out of the July 14 All-Star Game in Philadelphia; catcher Brian Serven recalled from Triple-A in a corresponding move.

item.title: Nick Lodolo (Baseball) — Blister on the left index finger (throwing hand); a recurring issue, his fourth stint on the IL for blisters dating back to 2024
item.description: Nick Lodolo (Cincinnati Reds (MLB)) — Blister on the left index finger (throwing hand); a recurring issue, his fourth stint on the IL for blisters dating back to 2024. Expected duration: Placed on the 15-day injured list; eligible to return July 27, though it could take longer depending on severity. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Reds turn to Rhett Lowder to fill Lodolo's rotation spot.

item.title: Bo Bichette (Baseball) — General leg soreness and right ankle soreness, aggravated by fouling balls off his legs during a series in Atlanta
item.description: Bo Bichette (New York Mets (MLB)) — General leg soreness and right ankle soreness, aggravated by fouling balls off his legs during a series in Atlanta. Expected duration: Held out of the lineup for a second straight game and available only off the bench; expected to use the following day and the All-Star break for a full reset rather than go on the injured list. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Mets manage his workload during a productive stretch (.320 avg, .788 OPS over his last 25 games) heading into the All-Star break.

item.title: Conor McGregor (Mixed Martial Arts) — Suspected torn ACL in the right knee, sustained throwing a kick just 69 seconds into his UFC 329 return bout; official diagnosis pending further medical evaluation
item.description: Conor McGregor (UFC) — Suspected torn ACL in the right knee, sustained throwing a kick just 69 seconds into his UFC 329 return bout; official diagnosis pending further medical evaluation. Expected duration: Fight stopped by the referee at 1:09 of round one; no recovery timeline given pending formal diagnosis. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Ends McGregor's much-hyped comeback fight against Max Holloway almost immediately and casts doubt on any near-term return to competition.

item.title: Ezi Magbegor (Basketball) — Facial fracture
item.description: Ezi Magbegor (Seattle Storm (WNBA)) — Facial fracture. Expected duration: Ruled out of the Storm's game vs. the Washington Mystics; to be reevaluated in the next couple of weeks. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Storm without their starting center/forward for at least the near term.

item.title: Konnor Griffin (Baseball) — Torn tendon (sagittal band) in the left ring finger, sustained diving for a ball; initially placed on the 10-day IL and now transferred to the 60-day IL
item.description: Konnor Griffin (Pittsburgh Pirates (MLB)) — Torn tendon (sagittal band) in the left ring finger, sustained diving for a ball; initially placed on the 10-day IL and now transferred to the 60-day IL. Expected duration: 8-10 week recovery timeline; ineligible to return until at least September 4; team opted for a non-operative approach, splinting the hand for about six weeks before resuming baseball activities. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Pirates lose their rookie starting shortstop (the No. 1 overall pick in the 2024 draft) for an extended stretch, his second significant injury of the season after an earlier forearm strain.

item.title: Collin Murray-Boyles (Basketball) — Sprained thumb/finger, diagnosed after exiting a Summer League game against the Lakers
item.description: Collin Murray-Boyles (Toronto Raptors (NBA) — Las Vegas Summer League) — Sprained thumb/finger, diagnosed after exiting a Summer League game against the Lakers. Expected duration: Ruled out of the Raptors' Summer League opener vs. Boston; next possible return targeted for the Houston game; full recovery timeline not specified in available reporting. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Raptors lose their 2025 first-round pick (9th overall) for early Summer League reps as a point-forward, part of his developmental push.

item.title: Shohei Ohtani (Baseball) — Left knee irritation/swelling, nursed since at least June 11
item.description: Shohei Ohtani (Los Angeles Dodgers (MLB)) — Left knee irritation/swelling, nursed since at least June 11. Expected duration: Scratched from his Friday, July 10 pitching start vs. Arizona and will skip the All-Star Game; scheduled to have the knee drained and possibly receive a pain-relieving injection during the break. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Dodgers manage the two-way star's workload heading into the second half; Ohtani continued to DH and homered the same day he was scratched from pitching.

item.title: Gout Gout (Athletics (Track & Field)) — Grade-three left hamstring tear suffered in training in Brisbane
item.description: Gout Gout (Athletics Australia (Track & Field)) — Grade-three left hamstring tear suffered in training in Brisbane. Expected duration: Ruled out for the remainder of the 2026 season, including the World Athletics U20 Championships (Aug. 5-9, Eugene, Oregon); targeting a return in 2027. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Removes the U20 200m world record holder from an anticipated showdown with American Tate Taylor at the World U20 Championships.

item.title: Harshit Rana (Cricket) — Hamstring injury sustained during the third T20I vs. England at Trent Bridge
item.description: Harshit Rana (India National Cricket Team) — Hamstring injury sustained during the third T20I vs. England at Trent Bridge. Expected duration: Ruled out of the remaining 4th and 5th T20Is of the series by the BCCI Medical Team. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Deepens India's bowling injury crisis on the England tour, with Rana joining Varun Chakaravarthy, Hardik Pandya, and Nitish Kumar Reddy on the unavailable list.

item.title: Varun Chakaravarthy (Cricket) — Hamstring injury sustained during the third T20I vs. England at Trent Bridge
item.description: Varun Chakaravarthy (India National Cricket Team) — Hamstring injury sustained during the third T20I vs. England at Trent Bridge. Expected duration: Ruled out of the remaining 4th and 5th T20Is of the series by the BCCI Medical Team. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: India's frontline wrist-spinner is unavailable as the team's injury list grows on the England tour.

item.title: Litton Das (Cricket) — Left calf injury; failed a fitness test ahead of the second ODI
item.description: Litton Das (Bangladesh national cricket team) — Left calf injury; failed a fitness test ahead of the second ODI. Expected duration: Ruled out of the remainder of the ODI series vs. Zimbabwe; continuing rehab in Dhaka. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Bangladesh loses their starting wicketkeeper-batter for the series; Parvez Hossain Emon named as replacement for the 2nd and 3rd ODIs.

item.title: Ravichandran Ashwin (Cricket) — Right knee injury
item.description: Ravichandran Ashwin (San Francisco Unicorns (Major League Cricket)) — Right knee injury. Expected duration: Ruled out for the remainder of MLC 2026; replaced by Peter Siddle. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Unicorns lose the veteran spinner after just one appearance this MLC season.

item.title: Aaron Judge (Baseball) — Stress fracture in right rib; has not played since May 31 and went on the injured list June 5
item.description: Aaron Judge (New York Yankees (MLB)) — Stress fracture in right rib; has not played since May 31 and went on the injured list June 5. Expected duration: Will have the rib reimaged during the All-Star break; no firm return date, GM Brian Cashman says he must be fully asymptomatic and show healing on imaging before resuming activity. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Yankees continue without their three-time AL MVP and captain in the middle of the lineup.

item.title: Mark Vientos (Baseball) — Fractured bone in right hand after being hit by a pitch from Michael Wacha
item.description: Mark Vientos (New York Mets (MLB)) — Fractured bone in right hand after being hit by a pitch from Michael Wacha. Expected duration: Headed to the injured list; broken bones of this type typically take around six weeks to heal, though the Mets have not given an official timeline and have not yet said whether surgery is needed. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Mets lose a regular infielder/DH (.211, 11 HR, 35 RBI in 72 games) just ahead of the trade deadline.

item.title: Zack Gelof (Baseball) — Deep cut underneath the right kneecap after colliding with the left field fence on a sliding catch; came just missed the tendon per the team
item.description: Zack Gelof (Athletics (MLB)) — Deep cut underneath the right kneecap after colliding with the left field fence on a sliding catch; came just missed the tendon per the team. Expected duration: Left the July 9 game early; scheduled for an MRI on July 10 to confirm the extent of the injury. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Athletics risk losing an everyday infielder/outfielder who had an on-base streak of 27 straight games and a 13-game scoring streak snapped by the injury.

item.title: Chinelle Henry (Cricket) — Exacerbation of a previous injury originally suffered fielding (stretchered off) in a T20 World Cup warm-up match in June
item.description: Chinelle Henry (West Indies Women's National Cricket Team) — Exacerbation of a previous injury originally suffered fielding (stretchered off) in a T20 World Cup warm-up match in June. Expected duration: Ruled out of the Women's ODI tour of Ireland beginning July 10; no return timeline disclosed. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: West Indies lose their vice-captain and a key all-rounder; Realeanna Grimmond called up as her replacement for the Ireland series.

item.title: Torstein Træen (Cycling) — Concussion and multiple fractured ribs sustained in a crash on the descent of the Col du Tourmalet during stage 6
item.description: Torstein Træen (Uno-X Mobility (Tour de France) — Norway) — Concussion and multiple fractured ribs sustained in a crash on the descent of the Col du Tourmalet during stage 6. Expected duration: Withdrew from the Tour de France (did not start stage 7); no return timeline specified in available reporting. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Uno-X Mobility loses the rider who had been wearing the race's yellow jersey after stage 5; Tadej Pogačar reclaimed the overall race lead the same day.

item.title: Alex Molenaar (Cycling) — Fracture of the first metacarpal in his right hand plus multiple contusions, sustained in a high-speed crash roughly 5km from the finish of stage 5; evaluated under concussion protocol after losing memory of the incident
item.description: Alex Molenaar (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA (Tour de France) — Netherlands) — Fracture of the first metacarpal in his right hand plus multiple contusions, sustained in a high-speed crash roughly 5km from the finish of stage 5; evaluated under concussion protocol after losing memory of the incident. Expected duration: Abandoned the Tour de France; did not start stage 6; recovery timeline not specified in available reporting. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Caja Rural-Seguros RGA loses a rider mid-Tour.

item.title: Arvid de Kleijn (Cycling) — Muscle tears sustained during the stage 1 team time trial, which hampered him for the rest of the race
item.description: Arvid de Kleijn (Tudor Pro Cycling Team (Tour de France) — Netherlands) — Muscle tears sustained during the stage 1 team time trial, which hampered him for the rest of the race. Expected duration: Abandoned the Tour de France on stage 6 after struggling since the opening days; recovery timeline not specified in available reporting. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Tudor Pro Cycling Team loses a rider mid-Tour after he battled to beat the time cut on multiple stages.

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz (Tennis) — Right-wrist tenosynovitis, forcing withdrawals from Madrid, Rome, Roland Garros, Queen's and Wimbledon; has worn a wrist brace since mid-April
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz (ATP Tour) — Right-wrist tenosynovitis, forcing withdrawals from Madrid, Rome, Roland Garros, Queen's and Wimbledon; has worn a wrist brace since mid-April. Expected duration: Has pulled out of the ATP Montreal entry list and opted to prolong his comeback by two more weeks, targeting Cincinnati or the US Open for his return; recently resumed light on-court practice without a brace. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: World No. 2 has now missed a full clay and grass swing including two Grand Slams; absence continues into the North American hard-court season.

item.title: Connor Bedard (Hockey) — Surgery on left shoulder after an injury sustained during a practice session in Vancouver on July 2
item.description: Connor Bedard (Chicago Blackhawks (NHL)) — Surgery on left shoulder after an injury sustained during a practice session in Vancouver on July 2. Expected duration: Expected to need approximately four months to recover; will miss training camp and at least the start of the 2026-27 season. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Blackhawks will open the 2026-27 season, played under the NHL's expanded 84-game schedule, without their No. 1 center for at least the opening month.

item.title: Aliyah Boston (Basketball) — Lower right leg injury
item.description: Aliyah Boston (Indiana Fever (WNBA)) — Lower right leg injury. Expected duration: Listed out for the Fever's game vs. the LA Sparks; expected to be available for the following game vs. Phoenix. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Fever forced to manage the availability of both Boston and Caitlin Clark during a back-to-back stretch; Boston, a voted-in 2026 All-Star starter, is having a career-best scoring season (17.1 ppg on 50.4% shooting) and was in danger of missing her first game since May 17.

item.title: Reece James (Soccer) — Hamstring injury sustained in England's final group-stage match vs. Ghana on June 23; described by reporting as worse than first thought
item.description: Reece James (Chelsea FC (Premier League) / England National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Hamstring injury sustained in England's final group-stage match vs. Ghana on June 23; described by reporting as worse than first thought. Expected duration: Touch-and-go for England's July 11 World Cup quarterfinal vs. Norway; has missed England's last three matches and was the sole absentee from the team's final group training session, requiring final medical clearance just to be considered for the bench. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Compounds an England right-back crisis that worsened after Jarell Quansah's red card vs. Mexico, leaving manager Thomas Tuchel with very limited options at the position for the quarterfinal.

item.title: Aurelien Tchouameni (Soccer) — Muscle tear involving the left adductor and upper thigh, a recurrence of the groin/thigh issue that previously sidelined him for France's group-stage match vs. Iraq
item.description: Aurelien Tchouameni (Real Madrid (La Liga) / France National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Muscle tear involving the left adductor and upper thigh, a recurrence of the groin/thigh issue that previously sidelined him for France's group-stage match vs. Iraq. Expected duration: Missed France's Round of 16 win over Paraguay; returned to partial team training on July 8, with a decision on his availability for the July 11 quarterfinal vs. Morocco pending. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: France's vice-captain and starting defensive midfielder is a doubt for the quarterfinal, with Manu Kone deputizing in his absence.

item.title: Mustafizur Rahman (Cricket) — Grade 1 muscle tear in the right hamstring and meniscal degeneration in the right knee, felt while bowling in the first ODI vs. Zimbabwe
item.description: Mustafizur Rahman (Bangladesh National Cricket Team) — Grade 1 muscle tear in the right hamstring and meniscal degeneration in the right knee, felt while bowling in the first ODI vs. Zimbabwe. Expected duration: Ruled out for four weeks, covering the remainder of Bangladesh's tour of Zimbabwe (concluding July 19), including the T20I series; has returned home for rehab. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Bangladesh lose a frontline pace bowler for the rest of the Zimbabwe tour.

item.title: Marco Raya (Baseball) — Right shoulder impingement
item.description: Marco Raya (Minnesota Twins (MLB)) — Right shoulder impingement. Expected duration: Placed on 15-day IL July 8, retroactive to July 5. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Twins lose a pitching option for at least 15 days.

item.title: Endy Rodriguez (Baseball) — Left glute strain
item.description: Endy Rodriguez (Pittsburgh Pirates (MLB)) — Left glute strain. Expected duration: Placed on 10-day IL July 8, retroactive to July 6. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Pirates lose their starting catcher for at least 10 days.

item.title: Taylor Fritz (Tennis) — Knee tendinitis flare-up, a recurring issue, three games into his Wimbledon quarterfinal
item.description: Taylor Fritz (ATP Tour) — Knee tendinitis flare-up, a recurring issue, three games into his Wimbledon quarterfinal. Expected duration: Played through the flare-up but lost the quarterfinal 6-4, 6-4, 6-2 to Alexander Zverev; no absence or further tournament withdrawal reported. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Eliminated from Wimbledon; dropped to No. 10 in the ATP Live Rankings.

item.title: Elvis Smylie (Golf) — Rib injury
item.description: Elvis Smylie (LIV Golf (Ripper GC) / DP World Tour) — Rib injury. Expected duration: Withdrew from the Genesis Scottish Open and The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale; announced an extended break from golf for rehabilitation, with no firm return timeline given. Financial impact: Forfeits prize money and Race to Dubai/world ranking points from both events. Team impact: Removes the Australian, who had qualified for his third Open Championship appearance, from the year's final men's major.

item.title: Jalen Brunson (Basketball) — Surgery to repair a left wrist injury that he played through during the Knicks' championship playoff run
item.description: Jalen Brunson (New York Knicks (NBA)) — Surgery to repair a left wrist injury that he played through during the Knicks' championship playoff run. Expected duration: At least two months of rehab expected; targeting readiness for the start of the 2026-27 NBA season. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Reigning Finals MVP and starting point guard sidelined for part of the offseason program, though expected back for training camp.

item.title: Amadou Onana (Soccer) — Torn anterior cruciate ligament (ACL), sustained in the 21st minute of Belgium's Round of 16 win over the United States after a challenge with Christian Pulisic
item.description: Amadou Onana (Aston Villa (Premier League) / Belgium National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Torn anterior cruciate ligament (ACL), sustained in the 21st minute of Belgium's Round of 16 win over the United States after a challenge with Christian Pulisic. Expected duration: Ruled out for the remainder of the World Cup; reported to be facing up to eight months out. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Belgium loses a key defensive midfielder ahead of their World Cup quarterfinal vs. Spain, and Aston Villa will be without him for a significant part of the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Giancarlo Stanton (Baseball) — Right calf strain (originally suffered running the bases on April 24 vs. the Astros); recent rehab setback after re-aggravating the calf during a running progression
item.description: Giancarlo Stanton (New York Yankees (MLB)) — Right calf strain (originally suffered running the bases on April 24 vs. the Astros); recent rehab setback after re-aggravating the calf during a running progression. Expected duration: No firm timeline; has not resumed running since the setback, no target return date given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Yankees continue without their veteran slugger amid an offense that has struggled with injuries; manager Aaron Boone still expects him back at some point in 2026.

item.title: Matteo Berrettini (Tennis) — Diagnosed with chronic hip pain, a recurrence of the left hip issue that forced his retirement from his Roland Garros quarterfinal in early June
item.description: Matteo Berrettini (ATP Tour) — Diagnosed with chronic hip pain, a recurrence of the left hip issue that forced his retirement from his Roland Garros quarterfinal in early June. Expected duration: Withdrawing from the ATP tournaments in Gstaad and Kitzbuhel to rest on medical advice, with the goal of being ready for the US hard-court swing culminating in the US Open; no firm return date given. Financial impact: Forfeits ranking points and prize money from both the Gstaad and Kitzbuhel events. Team impact: Removes him from the draw at both tournaments immediately following his third-round Wimbledon loss to Grigor Dimitrov.

item.title: Skylar Diggins (Basketball) — Right knee injury; Diggins has said a knee surgery from last October has contributed to ongoing strain
item.description: Skylar Diggins (Chicago Sky (WNBA)) — Right knee injury; Diggins has said a knee surgery from last October has contributed to ongoing strain. Expected duration: Ruled out for the July 7 game vs. Phoenix Mercury; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Comes as Diggins was moved to the bench for the first time in a decade, ending a streak of 341 consecutive starts; Sky beat Phoenix 77-66 in her absence.

item.title: Emma Raducanu (Tennis) — Stress fracture in lower right leg (ankle area); withdrew hours before her scheduled first-round match
item.description: Emma Raducanu (WTA Tour) — Stress fracture in lower right leg (ankle area); withdrew hours before her scheduled first-round match. Expected duration: Ruled out of Wimbledon 2026; still on crutches and wearing a protective boot several days after withdrawal, no return timeline given. Financial impact: Loses out on Wimbledon prize money and ranking points for the event. Team impact: Removes a top British draw from the tournament.

item.title: Jordan Henderson (Soccer) — Wrist fracture sustained in a freak fall while celebrating England's Round of 16 win over Mexico; reportedly requires surgery
item.description: Jordan Henderson (England National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Wrist fracture sustained in a freak fall while celebrating England's Round of 16 win over Mexico; reportedly requires surgery. Expected duration: Ruled out for the remainder of the 2026 World Cup; exact surgery date and recovery length not yet specified. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: England advance to the quarterfinals without an experienced veteran midfielder, compounding a growing injury list.

item.title: Santiago Gimenez (Soccer) — Grade II right ankle sprain suffered in stoppage time of Mexico's Round of 16 loss to England; hospitalized, but avoided fracture and ligament damage
item.description: Santiago Gimenez (AC Milan (Serie A) / Mexico National Team) — Grade II right ankle sprain suffered in stoppage time of Mexico's Round of 16 loss to England; hospitalized, but avoided fracture and ligament damage. Expected duration: Not yet specified by club or national team. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Injury compounds Mexico's World Cup elimination and raises concern over his fitness for AC Milan's preseason.

item.title: Ismael Saibari (Soccer) — Hamstring injury (minor muscle issue per medical tests; serious strain ruled out) suffered in the 22nd minute of Morocco's Round of 16 win over Canada
item.description: Ismael Saibari (Bayern Munich (Bundesliga, incoming) / Morocco National Team (2026 FIFA World Cup)) — Hamstring injury (minor muscle issue per medical tests; serious strain ruled out) suffered in the 22nd minute of Morocco's Round of 16 win over Canada. Expected duration: Day-to-day pending fitness test; final decision on availability for the World Cup quarterfinal vs. France expected only hours before kickoff. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Morocco risks losing their top attacking threat (three group-stage goals) for the biggest match of their World Cup run.

item.title: A'ja Wilson (Basketball) — Foot injury sustained in a game against the Chicago Sky
item.description: A'ja Wilson (Las Vegas Aces (WNBA)) — Foot injury sustained in a game against the Chicago Sky. Expected duration: Missed 3 games; coach Becky Hammon said she could have played if it were a playoff game, suggesting an imminent return. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Aces without their All-Star forward/center for a short stretch, including a loss to Indiana.

item.title: Caitlin Clark (Basketball) — Aggravated back injury
item.description: Caitlin Clark (Indiana Fever (WNBA)) — Aggravated back injury. Expected duration: Has resumed practicing; no confirmed timeline yet for a game return. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Fever have played without their All-Star starting guard for an extended stretch.

item.title: Napheesa Collier (Basketball) — Offseason surgery on both ankles
item.description: Napheesa Collier (Minnesota Lynx (WNBA)) — Offseason surgery on both ankles. Expected duration: Has yet to play in the 2026 season; recently resumed practicing. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Lynx have been without their All-Star forward for the entire season to date.

item.title: Lexi Thompson (Golf) — Persistent hip pain/suspected hip injury
item.description: Lexi Thompson (LPGA Tour) — Persistent hip pain/suspected hip injury. Expected duration: Withdrew from the KPMG Women's PGA Championship at Hazeltine National, after also sitting out the Meijer LPGA Classic with the same issue; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Ends a 15-tournament appearance streak at the Women's PGA Championship, a run that included four career top-10 finishes; Carolina Chacarra took her place in the field.

item.title: Byron Buxton (Baseball) — Aggravated hip injury while being caught stealing in the 1st inning vs. Yankees
item.description: Byron Buxton (Minnesota Twins (MLB)) — Aggravated hip injury while being caught stealing in the 1st inning vs. Yankees. Expected duration: Day-to-day; had already missed 4 games earlier in the week with the same hip issue. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Twins lose their 2026 All-Star outfielder from the lineup for an undetermined stretch.

item.title: Jazz Chisholm Jr. (Baseball) — Right toe discomfort, noticed jogging gingerly to first base after a fly out
item.description: Jazz Chisholm Jr. (New York Yankees (MLB)) — Right toe discomfort, noticed jogging gingerly to first base after a fly out. Expected duration: Being monitored day-to-day; no IL move confirmed yet. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Risk of losing Yankees' starting second baseman if it worsens.

item.title: Ranger Suarez (Baseball) — Left adductor tightness, pulled early from start vs. Angels
item.description: Ranger Suarez (Philadelphia Phillies (MLB)) — Left adductor tightness, pulled early from start vs. Angels. Expected duration: Undetermined, pending further evaluation. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Phillies could lose their 2026 AL All-Star-caliber starter from the rotation.

item.title: Brandon Woodruff (Baseball) — Right shoulder inflammation
item.description: Brandon Woodruff (Milwaukee Brewers (MLB)) — Right shoulder inflammation. Expected duration: Placed on 15-day Injured List (minimum). Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Brewers rotation loses a top starter for at least two full turns.

item.title: Triston Casas (Baseball) — Torn right patellar tendon (out since May); recently shut down from hitting progression
item.description: Triston Casas (Boston Red Sox (MLB)) — Torn right patellar tendon (out since May); recently shut down from hitting progression. Expected duration: Likely season-ending; setback in rehab timeline. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Red Sox lose starting first baseman for the remainder of the 2026 season.

item.title: Olivia Miles (Basketball) — Calf strain suffered during a July 3 game vs. the New York Liberty, possibly linked to in-game cramping
item.description: Olivia Miles (Minnesota Lynx (WNBA)) — Calf strain suffered during a July 3 game vs. the New York Liberty, possibly linked to in-game cramping. Expected duration: Ruled out of the Lynx's game vs. Connecticut on July 6, the first game of her WNBA career she has missed; no firm timetable given, though signs point to a quick recovery. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Costly absence for a Lynx team leaning on its rookie floor general; came a day after Miles was named a starter for the 2026 WNBA All-Star Game and while she remains a near-lock for Rookie of the Year (18.5 ppg, 4.8 rpg, 5.7 apg).

item.title: Serena Williams (Tennis) — Right knee injury tweaked at the end of the first set of her Wimbledon singles match against Maya Joint; postmatch video showed four syringes of fluid drained from the knee
item.description: Serena Williams (WTA Tour) — Right knee injury tweaked at the end of the first set of her Wimbledon singles match against Maya Joint; postmatch video showed four syringes of fluid drained from the knee. Expected duration: Withdrew from the Wimbledon doubles draw alongside sister Venus Williams; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Ends a highly anticipated comeback pairing with Venus Williams in doubles, coming during Serena's first singles match in nearly four years.

item.title: James Maddison (Soccer) — ACL reconstruction rehab (returning from tear)
item.description: James Maddison (Tottenham Hotspur (Premier League)) — ACL reconstruction rehab (returning from tear). Expected duration: Ongoing; currently restricted to 20-25 minutes per match. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Spurs managing his minutes carefully, limiting his impact as a starter.

item.title: Seamus Power (Golf) — Recurrence of long-standing back and hip issues he has dealt with since 2023
item.description: Seamus Power (PGA Tour) — Recurrence of long-standing back and hip issues he has dealt with since 2023. Expected duration: Withdrew after an opening-round 6-over 77 at the John Deere Classic on July 2; no return timeline given. Financial impact: Forfeits prize money and FedEx Cup points from the John Deere Classic. Team impact: Removes a two-time PGA Tour winner from the field partway through the event.

item.title: Nahikari Garcia (Soccer) — Season-ending knee (ACL) injury
item.description: Nahikari Garcia (Denver Summit FC (NWSL)) — Season-ending knee (ACL) injury. Expected duration: Out for the remainder of the 2026 NWSL season. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Denver Summit FC lose a starter for the rest of the season.

item.title: Brandon Ingram (Basketball) — Recovering from offseason surgery (lower body)
item.description: Brandon Ingram (Toronto Raptors (NBA)) — Recovering from offseason surgery (lower body). Expected duration: Expected back for training camp, fall 2026. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Raptors plan around his absence through the offseason program.

item.title: Josh Giddey (Basketball) — Arthroscopic surgery on right ankle
item.description: Josh Giddey (Chicago Bulls (NBA)) — Arthroscopic surgery on right ankle. Expected duration: ~3 months to resume basketball activities; targeting training camp. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Bulls lose a starting guard for the entire offseason program.

item.title: Corey Seager (Baseball) — Recurrence of lower back inflammation; his third injured list stint of the 2026 season (previously missed time with the same back issue and with a concussion)
item.description: Corey Seager (Texas Rangers (MLB)) — Recurrence of lower back inflammation; his third injured list stint of the 2026 season (previously missed time with the same back issue and with a concussion). Expected duration: Placed on the 10-day injured list. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Rangers lose their star shortstop again, continuing a season of infield instability; Josh Smith recalled from Triple-A as replacement.

item.title: Brent Rooker (Baseball) — Cartilage tear in left knee, discovered during an examination at Stanford; requires season-ending surgery
item.description: Brent Rooker (Athletics (MLB)) — Cartilage tear in left knee, discovered during an examination at Stanford; requires season-ending surgery. Expected duration: Season-ending; hadn't played since June 8 and had been on the injured list since June 9. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Athletics lose their two-time All-Star middle-of-the-order bat for the remainder of the 2026 season.

item.title: JuJu Watkins (Basketball) — Recovering from a torn ACL that cost her the entire 2025-26 season
item.description: JuJu Watkins (USC Trojans (NCAA Division I Women's Basketball)) — Recovering from a torn ACL that cost her the entire 2025-26 season. Expected duration: Returned to the court roughly 14 months after the injury. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: USC gets back its star guard after a full season lost to the injury.

item.title: Donnie Freeman (Basketball) — Torn right Achilles tendon, a non-contact injury suffered during a summer workout; underwent successful surgery performed by Dr. Martin O'Malley
item.description: Donnie Freeman (St. John's University (NCAA Division I Men's Basketball)) — Torn right Achilles tendon, a non-contact injury suffered during a summer workout; underwent successful surgery performed by Dr. Martin O'Malley. Expected duration: Will miss the entire 2026-27 season. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Major setback for coach Rick Pitino and St. John's, who added the rising junior forward as a transfer-portal pickup this offseason; Freeman averaged 16.5 points and 7.2 rebounds last season at Syracuse.

item.title: Jaren Jackson Jr. (Basketball) — Turf toe surgery (right foot)
item.description: Jaren Jackson Jr. (Memphis Grizzlies (NBA)) — Turf toe surgery (right foot). Expected duration: Re-evaluation in approximately 12 weeks. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Grizzlies lose their All-Star big man through the offseason and possibly into training camp.

item.title: Manuel Ugarte (Soccer) — Knee ligament injury suffered vs. Spain at the World Cup; possible cruciate ligament (ACL) damage
item.description: Manuel Ugarte (Manchester United (Premier League) / Uruguay National Team) — Knee ligament injury suffered vs. Spain at the World Cup; possible cruciate ligament (ACL) damage. Expected duration: Potentially several months if ACL is confirmed. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Would be a significant blow to Manchester United's midfield depth heading into 2026-27.

item.title: Rodri (Soccer) — Undisclosed injury expected to require surgery after the World Cup
item.description: Rodri (Manchester City (Premier League)) — Undisclosed injury expected to require surgery after the World Cup. Expected duration: Out for the opening weeks of the 2026-27 season. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Manchester City to start the new season without their midfield anchor.

item.title: Kelsey Plum (Basketball) — Lower leg injury
item.description: Kelsey Plum (Los Angeles Sparks (WNBA)) — Lower leg injury. Expected duration: Ruled out for at least 4 weeks as of late June; All-Star Game (July 25) status still to be decided. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Sparks lose a starting guard through their All-Star break stretch.

item.title: Seth Jarvis (Hockey) — Shoulder surgery, the same shoulder that had a torn labrum and rotator cuff during the 2023-24 season
item.description: Seth Jarvis (Carolina Hurricanes (NHL)) — Shoulder surgery, the same shoulder that had a torn labrum and rotator cuff during the 2023-24 season. Expected duration: Expected recovery of approximately 4-6 months, meaning the earliest return would be late October. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Hurricanes will be without a top-six forward for the start of the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Bradley Beal (Basketball) — Hip fracture requiring season-ending surgery
item.description: Bradley Beal (LA Clippers (NBA)) — Hip fracture requiring season-ending surgery. Expected duration: Season-ending; recovery expected to extend into 2026-27 preseason. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Clippers lose a veteran guard for the balance of the season and into next year's planning.

item.title: Bejlek (Tennis) — Elbow injury
item.description: Bejlek (WTA Tour) — Elbow injury. Expected duration: Withdrew mid-tournament from Eastbourne. Financial impact: Forfeits Eastbourne prize money/points from the round of withdrawal. Team impact: Opponent Zeynep Sonmez advances by walkover.

item.title: Matthijs de Ligt (Soccer) — Surgery on a long-standing back injury
item.description: Matthijs de Ligt (Manchester United (Premier League)) — Surgery on a long-standing back injury. Expected duration: Will miss the 2026 World Cup; club return timeline unconfirmed. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: United lose a starting center-back for an extended stretch.

item.title: Elina Svitolina (Tennis) — Hip injury
item.description: Elina Svitolina (WTA Tour) — Hip injury. Expected duration: Withdrew before her Bad Homburg quarterfinal. Financial impact: Forfeits Bad Homburg quarterfinal prize money/points. Team impact: Opponent Xinyu Wang advances by walkover.

item.title: Chris Richards (Soccer) — Torn ankle ligaments (two ligaments) suffered vs. Brentford
item.description: Chris Richards (Crystal Palace (Premier League) / USMNT) — Torn ankle ligaments (two ligaments) suffered vs. Brentford. Expected duration: Expected to recover in time for the 2026 World Cup. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Crystal Palace lose a starting center-back for a multi-week stretch.

item.title: Chloe Ricketts (Soccer) — Ankle injury
item.description: Chloe Ricketts (Boston Legacy FC (NWSL)) — Ankle injury. Expected duration: Placed on the 45-day short-term injury list. Financial impact: Not disclosed in available reporting. Team impact: Boston Legacy FC without a rostered defender for at least 45 days.
